subject: English / First Flight
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the imagery and personification used in 'The Trees'
Understand the themes of movement, freedom, and nature
Interpret the poet’s reflection on human observation versus natural action
Develop summarization and analytical writing skills
Encourage creative expression with descriptive writing and visualizations

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Adrienne Rich and the Poem
topics_covered: Introduction to key literary devices: imagery, personification
teaching_strategy: Briefly explain Adrienne Rich’s poetic style and focus on nature
Discuss students’ observations of trees and forests
student_activities: Predict what the poem might describe based on the title
Share experiences of noticing nature and changes in the environment
assessment: Oral Q&A on the poet and poem

lesson_plan.title: Detailed Reading and Comprehension
topics_covered: Explain difficult words: veranda, lichen, disengage, whispers
Identify the movement of trees and their personification
teaching_strategy: Read stanza by stanza, pause to explain imagery and metaphors
Encourage students to visualize the trees’ actions and the forest setting
student_activities: Underline phrases showing movement, freedom, and human-like qualities of trees
assessment: Check understanding through oral questions on imagery and meaning
Review summaries for comprehension and clarity

lesson_plan.title: Themes and Symbolism
topics_covered: Movement of trees as a symbol of freedom and autonomy
Contrast between human observation and natural processes
The role of night, moon, and sensory elements in creating mood
teaching_strategy: Discuss the deeper meanings behind the trees’ actions
Connect imagery to themes of independence and natural life cycles
student_activities: List symbols and metaphors in the poem
Explain in their own words how the poet conveys freedom and life
assessment: Oral Q&A on symbolism and themes
Check written explanations for understanding of deeper meanings

lesson_plan.title: Analytical Writing
topics_covered: Writing a short analysis of imagery, personification, and themes
teaching_strategy: Guide students to write 5–6 sentence paragraphs analyzing one aspect of the poem
Encourage linking imagery to themes and poet’s message
student_activities: Write a paragraph analyzing the movement of trees and human observation
Share and discuss their paragraphs in pairs or groups
assessment: Evaluate analytical writing for clarity, depth, and textual references
Provide feedback on linking imagery to theme

lesson_plan.title: Creative Expression and Reflection
topics_covered: Expressing the poem visually or creatively
Reflecting on the interaction between humans and nature
teaching_strategy: Encourage creative visualization and descriptive writing
Discuss how observing nature can inspire reflection and art
student_activities: Draw or paint a scene from the poem showing trees moving into the forest
Write a short reflective paragraph about personal experiences with nature or observation
assessment: Evaluate creativity, connection to the poem, and observation skills
Class discussion on interpretations and reflections

teaching_aids: Printed or projected text of 'The Trees'
Images of forests, trees, moonlit nights, and roots
Charts highlighting imagery, symbolism, and themes
Flashcards with difficult words and literary terms
evaluation_methods: Creative drawing or descriptive writing activities
Group discussion on imagery, symbolism, and themes
teacher_tip: Focus on helping students interpret imagery and symbolism. Emphasize the trees’ movement as a metaphor for freedom and autonomy. Encourage students to relate observations in the poem to their own experiences with nature.
